---
title: "Interactive " 
description: "Debugging Matplotlib backends in a terminal-based workflow using IPython and QtAgg"
date: 2026-04-18
categories: [Sentinel-2, Python, Remote Sensing]
licence: "CC BY-NC"
image: img/portada.png
jupyter: python3
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## The setup 

I'm using nvim + quarto for my daily analysis. Although I could use the
render option to check the quarto output, I rather to plot some of the 
intermediate results and check the results/images for validation. 


But, when using the terminal there is no way to show interactively a plot, 
unless you save the figure, and open it. But that means moving away from my 
terminal. 

One solution is to use QtAgg to setup the matplotlib [backend](https://matplotlib.org/stable/users/explain/figure/backends.html#backends)

The setup that worked with my workflow is: 

## The steps

At the start of any project I use uv to setup the env: 

```{bash}
uv init

```

## The file 

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("QtAgg")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
```


```{python}
img = np.random.rand(100, 100, 3)

plt.imshow(img)
plt.axis("off")
plt.title("Random image")
plt.show()
```
